items.title: OpenAI pushes harder into health triage
items.oneliner: OpenAI says 230 million ask ChatGPT health questions weekly, and its reasoning model surfaced 18 rare diseases from unsolved cases.

items.title: Perplexity adds self-improving agent memory
items.oneliner: Perplexity says Brain for Computer lifted answer correctness 25 percent, making long-running agents look less stateless and brittle.

items.title: Anthropic simplifies enterprise MCP authentication
items.oneliner: Anthropic added enterprise-managed auth for MCP, removing per-connector OAuth pain that has slowed serious internal agent deployments.

items.title: Anthropic quietly broadens privacy disclosure latitude
items.oneliner: Anthropic's revised privacy policy allows disclosures based on internal assessment, which should unsettle anyone sending sensitive enterprise context.

items.title: MIT built an OS for chip forensics
items.oneliner: MIT's Fractal kernel exposed Phantom speculative flaws in Apple's M1, showing hardware security research now needs purpose-built operating systems.

items.title: Honey's affiliate hijack gets a clearer autopsy
items.oneliner: Honey allegedly replaced creators' affiliate cookies with its own, a reminder that browser extensions can quietly reroute revenue at scale.

items.title: AI memory demand is hitting hardware prices
items.oneliner: Apple warned AI data-center memory demand could raise iPhone and Mac prices, linking model scaling directly to consumer hardware inflation.

items.title: American Express details cell-based payments architecture
items.oneliner: American Express says cell-based architecture shrinks payment failure blast radius, which is the infrastructure pattern more AI systems will copy.

items.title: Datasette turns SQL backends into app hosts
items.oneliner: Datasette Apps lets teams ship sandboxed HTML frontends over live SQL, a sharp shortcut for internal tools and data products.

items.title: Extend UI targets document-heavy product teams
items.oneliner: Extend UI packages React components for PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, uploads, and e-signing, cutting boilerplate for document workflows.
